data.name: Argument_EmptyOrWhiteSpaceString
data.value: The value cannot be an empty string or composed entirely of whitespace.

data.name: Argument_EmptyString
data.value: The value cannot be an empty string.

data.name: Argument_EmptyCollection
data.value: The collection must contain at least one element.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeNonZero
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be a non-zero value.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeNonNegative
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be a non-negative value.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeNonNegativeNonZero
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be a non-negative and non-zero value.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeLessOrEqual
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be less than or equal to '{2}'.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeLess
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be less than '{2}'.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeGreaterOrEqual
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be greater than or equal to '{2}'.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeGreater
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be greater than '{2}'.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeEqual
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must be equal to '{2}'.

data.name: ArgumentOutOfRange_Generic_MustBeNotEqual
data.value: {0} ('{1}') must not be equal to '{2}'.

data.name: PathMustBeRooted
data.value: Path must be rooted.

data.name: PathTooLong
data.value: Path: {0} exceeds the OS max path limit. The fully qualified file name must be less than {1} characters.

data.name: FailedCreatingTempFile
data.value: Failed to create a temporary file. Temporary files folder is full or its path is incorrect. {0}

data.name: InvalidGetPathOfFileAboveParameter
data.value: The parameter '{0}' can only be a file name and cannot include a directory.

data.name: LogFileOutputPath
data.value: {0} wrote to: {1}
data.comment: {0} is the logger name. {1} is a list of full file paths separated by the current culture's list separator (followed by a space).

data.name: DebugPathTooLong
data.value: The path "{0}" used for debug logs is too long. Set it to a shorter value using the MSBUILDDEBUGPATH environment variable or change your system configuration to allow long paths.

data.name: InvalidFilespecForTransform
data.value: The item metadata "%({0})" cannot be applied to the path "{1}". {2}
data.comment: UE: This message is shown when the user tries to perform path manipulations using one of the built-in item metadata e.g. %(RootDir), on an item-spec that's not a valid path. LOCALIZATION: "{2}" is a localized message from a CLR/FX exception.

data.name: CollectionIsReadOnly
data.value: This collection is read-only.

data.name: CannotConvertStringToBool
data.value: The string "{0}" cannot be converted to a boolean (true/false) value.

data.name: InvalidAttributeMetadata
data.value: Item "{0}" has attribute "{1}" with value "{2}" that could not be converted to "{3}".

data.name: GlobExpansionFailed
data.value: An exception occurred while expanding a fileSpec with globs: fileSpec: "{0}", assuming it is a file name. Exception: {1}

data.name: UnsupportedFrameworkVersionForWindowsSdk
data.value: .NET Framework version "{0}" is not supported when explicitly targeting the Windows SDK, which is only supported on .NET 4.5 and later. Please specify a value from the enumeration Microsoft.Build.Utilities.TargetDotNetFrameworkVersion that is Version45 or above.

data.name: UnsupportedVisualStudioVersion
data.value: Visual Studio version "{0}" is not supported. Please specify a value from the enumeration Microsoft.Build.Utilities.VisualStudioVersion.

data.name: UnsupportedFrameworkVersion
data.value: .NET Framework version "{0}" is not supported. Please specify a value from the enumeration Microsoft.Build.Utilities.TargetDotNetFrameworkVersion.

data.name: CouldNotGenerateReferenceAssemblyDirectory
data.value: When attempting to generate a reference assembly path from the path "{0}" and the framework moniker "{1}" there was an error. {2}

data.name: _0_does_not_contain_matching_element
data.value: '{0}' does not contain matching element.

data.name: _0_contains_no_elements
data.value: '{0}' contains no elements.

data.name: CollectionCopyToFailureProvidedArrayIsTooSmall
data.value: The number of elements in the collection is greater than the available space in the destination array (when starting at the specified index).

data.name: ParameterCannotHaveInvalidPathChars
data.value: Parameter "{0}" with assigned value "{1}" cannot have invalid path or invalid file characters.

data.name: ConflictingTaskAssembly
data.value: MSB4008: A conflicting assembly for the task assembly "{0}" has been found at "{1}".
data.comment: {StrBegin="MSB4008: "}UE: This message is shown when the type/class of a task cannot be resolved uniquely from a single assembly.

data.name: ExpectedEventToBeSerializable
data.value: Event type "{0}" was expected to be serializable using the .NET serializer. The event was not serializable and has been ignored.

data.name: UnrecognizedToolsVersion
data.value: MSB4132: The tools version "{0}" is unrecognized. Available tools versions are {1}.

data.name: NameInvalid
data.value: MSB5016: The name "{0}" contains an invalid character "{1}".

data.name: CannotChangeItemSpecModifiers
data.value: "{0}" is a reserved item metadata, and cannot be modified or deleted.
data.comment: UE: Tasks and OM users are not allowed to remove or change the value of the built-in metadata on items e.g. the meta-data "FullPath", "RelativeDir", etc. are reserved.

data.name: TaskNotMarshalByRef
data.value: MSB4077: The "{0}" task has been marked with the attribute LoadInSeparateAppDomain, but does not derive from MarshalByRefObject. Check that the task derives from MarshalByRefObject or AppDomainIsolatedTask.

data.name: TaskResourceNotFound
data.value: The resource string "{0}" for the "{1}" task cannot be found. Confirm that the resource name "{0}" is correctly spelled, and the resource exists in the task's assembly.

data.name: TaskResourcesNotRegistered
data.value: The "{0}" task has not registered its resources. In order to use the "TaskLoggingHelper.FormatResourceString()" method this task needs to register its resources either during construction, or via the "TaskResources" property.
data.comment: LOCALIZATION: "TaskLoggingHelper.FormatResourceString()" and "TaskResources" should not be localized.

data.name: WildcardResultsInDriveEnumeration
data.value: MSB5029: The value "{0}" of the "{1}" attribute in element <{2}> in file "{3}" is a wildcard that results in enumerating all files on the drive, which was likely not intended. Check that referenced properties are always defined and that the project and current working directory are not at the drive root.
data.comment: {StrBegin="MSB5029: "}UE: This is a generic message that is displayed when we find a project element that has a drive enumerating wildcard value for one of its attributes e.g. <Compile Include="$(NotAlwaysDefined)\**\*.cs"> -- if the property is undefined, the value of Include should not result in enumerating all files on drive.

data.name: LoggingBeforeTaskInitialization
data.value: MSB6005: Task attempted to log before it was initialized. Message was: {0}
data.comment: {StrBegin="MSB6005: "}UE: This occurs if the task attempts to log something in its own constructor.

data.name: Message_InvalidImportance
data.value: MSB3511: "{0}" is an invalid value for the "Importance" parameter. Valid values are: High, Normal and Low.
data.comment: {StrBegin="MSB3511: "}UE: This message is shown when a user specifies a value for the importance attribute of Message which is not valid. The importance enumeration is: High, Normal and Low. Specifying any other importance will result in this message being shown LOCALIZATION: "Importance" should not be localized. High should not be localized. Normal should not be localized. Low should not be localized.
